facts.text: Indian River Shores sits on Orchid Island, a barrier island that separates the Atlantic Ocean from the Indian River Lagoon.
The town’s streets are dominated by gated, low‑density residential communities, with Florida State Road A1A as the main public roadway.
Indian River Shores is part of the Sebastian‑Vero Beach‑West Vero Corridor Metropolitan Area, the northernmost segment of the South Florida Combined Statistical Area.
To the north lies Wabasso Beach, to the south Vero Beach, and across the Indian River to the west are Winter Beach and Gifford, while the Atlantic Ocean borders the east.
In 2022, the town ranked tenth in Florida for per‑capita income, reflecting its affluent residential character.
The official town website promises to make Indian River Shores the most livable and best‑managed community through courteous, quality services and teamwork.
